events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer raw JSON only. Need parse scenario: commercial construction, motor circuits & disconnects, staged. Observed field condition: undersized motor overloads. Work is staged. Reference NEC Art. 430. Need select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice wrong. We must output JSON with fields. Decision likely fail because non-compliant and potential hazard. Risk maybe high/critical. Need fill values reasonably. Need include references to NEC Art. 430, specifically 430.32 (Motor and branch-circuit overload protection), 430.6 (ampacity and motor rating), 430.22 (branch circuit conductors), maybe 430.110 (disconnect rating). Need correct approach: replace/adjust overload relays/heaters with proper size per NEC 430.32 based on motor full-load current (nameplate/Table) and service factor/temperature. Use manufacturer's heater selection chart. Wrong because undersized overloads cause nuisance tripping or inadequate protection? Actually undersized overloads would trip too soon causing nuisance and potential motor damage? The hazard: if overload setting below FLA, false trips; if improperly sized below 125% of FLA? Wait NEC says overload setting not exceed 140% of full-load current for motors with marked service factor 1.15+ or temp rise <=40°C; 130% for all others. If undersized below motor FLA, it can trip nuisance; but from compliance standpoint overloads must be sized per Art 430. Undersized overloads also may not provide proper running protection? Under NEC, overloads must trip at not more than those percentages; too low is not code violation for max but could be impractical. However observed choice wrong because materials staged are incorrect: undersized overload heaters/relays won't match motor FLA and will trip erroneously, and may fail inspection. Need use correct replacement approach. Maybe the correct overload device/heater must be selected using nameplate FLA and ambient correction per manufacturer.

Need fill all fields.

decision: fail. risk: high (or critical?). Work_stage: staged. component_conditions: ["installed-defective","non-compliant"]. percent_complete: 0? Staged work not begun. Maybe 10. value: 0. sound_source: "". confidence: 0.95. event_time:0, rate:0, order empty, workable empty. findings: ["Overload heaters/relays are undersized for the motor full-load current", "Does not meet NEC Art. 430.32 sizing requirements", "Will cause nuisance tripping and may fail inspection"]. actions: ["Replace overload heaters/relays with correct size based on motor nameplate FLA and NEC 430.32", "Verify overload selection with manufacturer heater chart", "Resubmit correct overload schedule for review/inspection"]. rationale: brief explanation. references: ["NEC Art. 430.32", "NEC Art. 430.6", "NEC Art. 430.110"] maybe.

Need ensure JSON shape exactly. No prose outside JSON. We are to write to /app/answer.json? In this chat, we respond raw JSON. They said don't include prose. So output only JSON.

Let's craft.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "staged",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Overload heaters/relays staged for the motor circuit are undersized for the motor full-load current",
    "Sizing does not comply with NEC Article 430 motor overload protection requirements",
    "Undersized overloads will nuisance-trip and will not provide the required running protection profile",
    "Condition will likely fail third-party inspection and submittal review"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the undersized overload heaters/relays from the staging area",
    "Select replacement overload heaters/relays based on the motor nameplate full-load current using NEC 430.32 limits",
    "Use the motor starter manufacturer's heater selection chart and correct any ambient-temperature adjustment",
    "Update the motor schedule/submittal and request re-inspection before installation"
  ],
  "rationale": "Motor overload protection must be sized to the motor full-load current per NEC Article 430, typically not more than 115%-140% of FLA depending on motor service factor and temperature rating. Undersized overloads trip below normal running current, causing nuisance shutdowns, and fail to meet the required protection and inspection criteria.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Article 430, Part III — Motor and Branch-Circuit Overload Protection",
    "NEC 430.32 — Continuous-Duty Motors",
    "NEC 430.6 — Determining Motor Full-Load Current",
    "NEC 430.110 — Ampere Rating and Motor Disconnects"
  ]
}
